rulings.Case Title: JOHN BAUER et al VS. ST. FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 26, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANT KENNETH LIGHT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment and/or summary adjudication is denied. Triable issue of fact raised whether defendant breached the standard of care. (Dr. Uppal declaration and medical records produced by defendant. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL RATNER et al VS. 1314 POLK STREET ASSOCIATES, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 26, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT IAN BIRCHALL DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to 3rd, 4th, 7th, 8th and 10th causes of action. Facts sufficiently pled to support alter ego allegations which will be the subject of discovery. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 9th cause of action for unjust enrichment which is not a cause of action but rather a principle underlying certain doctrines and remedies. See Jogani vs. Superior Court (2008) 165 Cal.App. 4th 901, 911. Although motion to strike not properly separately noticed (CRC 3.1322), motion granted with ten days leave to amend as to attorneys fees for plaintiffs to specify which defendants they are seeking fees from and what basis; and to allege facts amounting to fraudulent, oppressive, and malicious conduct. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARK HARPER VS. CHARLES SCHWAB & CO., INC et al

rulings.Case Title: CAPURRO PROPERTIES LLC VS. GENE BAKER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 26, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANT GENE BAKAR MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion denied. Triable issue of fact regarding parties' agreement when partial payment made. As a matter of law, code does not require notice to be dated, nor the identity of the person who issued the notice. =(302/CWW)
